statistics.name: assists
statistics.description: The number of times a player who passes the ball to a teammate in a way that leads to a score by field goal, meaning that he or she was "assisting" in the basket. There is some judgment involved in deciding whether a passer should be credited with an assist.

statistics.description: The number of times a player loses possession to the other team.

articles.headline: Las Vegas takes on New York after Young's 21-point game
articles.description: New York Liberty (11-6, 8-1 Eastern Conference) at Las Vegas Aces (12-4, 9-4 Western Conference)

categories.description: New York Liberty @ Las Vegas Aces

event.description: New York Liberty @ Las Vegas Aces

articles.headline: Ogwumike's 3-pointer at the buzzer lifts Sparks past Liberty
articles.description: Nneka Ogwumike's 3-pointer at the buzzer sent the Sparks past the Liberty on Sunday night in a rematch of the first WNBA game in 1997.

images.name: sparks.jpg photodesk [600x400]
images.caption: Nneka Ogwumike of the Los Angeles Sparks dribbles the ball during the game against the New York Liberty on June 21, 2026 at Crypto.com Arena in Los Angeles, California.
images.alt: Ogwumike dribbles and pushes back against a Liberty player with ref watching from back left.

images.name: Nneka Ogwumike calls game with buzzer-beating 3 for Sparks
images.caption: Nneka Ogwumike calls game with buzzer-beating 3 for Sparks

articles.headline: Nneka Ogwumike hits a buzzer-beating 3 as the Sparks beat the Liberty 98-97
articles.description: — Nneka Ogwumike hit a 3-pointer at the buzzer to lift the Los Angeles Sparks to a 98-97 victory over the New York Liberty on Sunday night on the anniversary of the league's first game...

images.name: Nneka Ogwumike fights back tears in emotional postgame interview

articles.headline: Mystics use a late 12-0 surge to beat Lynx 84-79 and win third straight game
articles.description: — Sonia Citron had 21 points and Kiki Iriafen added 17, hitting a go-ahead basket late for a second straight game, to help the Washington Mystics beat the Minnesota Lynx 84-79 on Sunday night.

articles.headline: Aces surge past Valkyries 92-73 as Jackie Young shines in a 21-2 run
articles.description: — Jackie Young scored 21 points and A'Ja Wilson added 19 points and nine rebounds to help the Las Vegas Aces cruise to a 92-73 victory over the Golden State Valkyries on Sunday.

articles.headline: Sun take on the Sky on 7-game losing streak
articles.description: Chicago Sky (4-11, 1-6 Eastern Conference) at Connecticut Sun (2-15, 0-8 Eastern Conference)

articles.headline: Seattle faces Dallas, seeks to break 10-game skid
articles.description: Dallas Wings (10-6, 5-4 Western Conference) at Seattle Storm (3-14, 0-9 Western Conference)

articles.headline: Clark leads Indiana against Phoenix after 26-point game
articles.description: Phoenix Mercury (5-12, 4-8 Western Conference) at Indiana Fever (9-7, 5-4 Eastern Conference)

articles.headline: Bueckers fuels rally as Wings erase 15-point deficit in 4th
articles.description: The Wings exploded for a season-high 36 fourth-quarter points to rally from a 15-point deficit and stun the Sky 93-92.

articles.headline: Li Yueru hits 2 late free throws as the Wings rally past the Sky 93-92
articles.description: — Li Yueru made two free throws with 12.5 seconds left to give Dallas its first lead as the Wings scored a season high 36 fourth-quarter points to rally from a 14-point deficit to beat the Chicago Sky 93-92 on Saturday night.

images.name: Paige Bueckers, Li Yueru spark Wings' comeback to shock Sky
images.caption: Paige Bueckers, Li Yueru spark Wings' comeback to shock Sky

article.description: — Brittney Sykes scored 25 points, Nyara Sabally added 15 and the Toronto Tempo beat the Chicago Sky 85-68 on Sunday.

tracking.trackingName: COM_WNBA Highlight (Tempo win big at home over the Sky) 2026/06/07 ESHEET

article.story: TORONTO -- — Brittney Sykes scored 25 points, Nyara Sabally added 15 and the Toronto Tempo beat the Chicago Sky 85-68 on Sunday. Isabelle Harrison, who missed the first 10 games of the season with a thumb injury, finished with 14 points in her season debut and Laura Juskaite scored 10 for the Tempo (6-5). Marina Mabrey added nine points, six assists, four rebounds, two steals and a block. Azura Stevens had a season-high 18 points and 10 rebounds for Chicago, her second consecutive game with a double-double. Gabriela Jaquez (knee) returned from a four-game absence and finished with 11 points, and Jacy Sheldon scored 10. The Sky (4-7) beat Connecticut 85-80 on Friday to snap a five-game skid. Harrison hit a 3-pointer with 4:12 left in the first quarter that made it 20-9 and the Tempo led by double figures the rest of the way. The Tempo set season lows for 3-pointers made (four) and 3-point percentage (18.2%). Rookie Kiki Rice (ankle) did not play for Toronto. Toronto beat the Sky 111-104 behind a career-high 29 points from Sabally on Friday in Chicago. Up next Sky: Host Atlanta on Tuesday. Tempo: Host Connecticut on Wednesday. ------ AP WNBA: https://apnews.com/hub/wnba-basketball
